Hey JB here, Tom here daily motivation slash skills training. Just visited my dad in the hospital. He's actually doing really well. He's a bounce back king. So, you know, we'll see what happens. He's definitely got a little time left. So which is good news. You know, I was thinking this morning about the story I always used to tell about ducks and eagles. And it's one of the ways I would explain standards. It's people with high standards, you know, people with those eagles that's sore, then you have the ducks in the world who, you know, basically have this impossibility notion. And they just are quack, quack, quack it around in some duck palm with all your ducks about why they can't get what they want and settling for less than they can't beloved and, you know, just doing just enough to get by. You know what the duck like mentality is, you know, it's like, if you're doing a job, even if you don't get paid extra for doing a great job, okay, the duck like mentality being, well, since I'm not getting paid extra, I'm just going to do the least bit possible to get by. You get it? There's no idea that I just have a pride in everything I do versus the eagle. They will always just, even if they're not getting paid extra based on like extraordinary performance, they feel the need to perform at an extraordinary level just when they live. And what happens, the reason I bring this up is because what happens is that even in situations when there's nothing at stake, you have nothing to gain by doing something at the highest level, it actually builds muscle and gets you into the habit of just always, you know, seizing the moment, taking action and applying yourself, whatever you do at the level that you really are truly capable versus enough to just get by. And that's really what Stamina's is about. It's about like, it's like, do you go through life just getting by or do you try to hit the bell on everything you do really, bam, just to get it right on the nose? And, you know, yes, well, I say this, this standards for everything, right? You have standards for money, you have standards for happiness, you have standards for your relationship with God or nature, whichever you believe in, doesn't matter, right? But the point is, is that while there are standards for everything, the one commonality to everything when it comes to standards is just your own pride in a job well done. Like whatever you do, whether it's your vacuuming the floor, making your bed, painting a fence, becoming a world-class closer, mastering entrepreneurship, you know, taking care of someone you love, you could go about it at an ordinary level, or you could push yourself to, now the operative order is to try, to try to hit the bell, to try to succeed at the highest level. That doesn't mean you're always going to do it. And that's the key here. You see, you know, when I'm not saying that I go through life and get everything right or I always hit it exactly on the nose, that's not what I mean. But I try, you know, either I don't do something or I do it to the absolute best of my ability. And I don't care what it is. If I'm going to love, I'm going to love fully. If I'm going to be in a friendship with someone, I'm going to be a great friend at the highest level. If I'm going to build a business, I'm going to build the biggest business. Think about your own life and just remember that you live once. What is the point of going through all this if you're not just going at life every single day and really striving to just hit it on the nose with everything you do? I don't care what it is. You know, at some level, yeah, I always teach people like the different types of standards, but the more I think about it, I think the only way to really ensure success in your life is to just become the sort of person that always gives it your all no matter what you do. What don't do it? Don't take half measures. You know, whatever you do, embrace it. If you're going to eat food, enjoy eating your food. You know, if you're going to go shop and go buy something that's going to make you really happy, stop settling for average. Seriously, just stop settling for things that you buy, things you move as you enjoy. If you're going to laugh, laugh. If you want to feel pain, then you're going to feel pain. Let yourself feel pain. I don't mean physical pain. I'm talking about emotional pain if you're in a loss over a relationship. Okay? Laugh, cry, love, feel. The point is that if you go through life numbing yourself and dummy, it's numbing and dumbing yourself down and not insisting that you at least try to hit it at the highest level. You set yourself up for the slow kind of descent or settling point at mediocrity. Don't do that. I want you to try to make it a habit of just whatever action you take today and the day after the day after. Just do it with every bit that you got or don't do it. And that's okay too, but don't do it halfway. If you're going to do it, do it. All right? We'll be all talking tomorrow.
